Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 SINCGARS Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 30 - 87.99500 MHz, 5.0 kHz Steps 30 - 87.99375 MHz, 6.25 kHz Steps 30 - 87.99346 MHz, 8.33 kHz Steps 30 - 87.98750 MHz, 12.5 kHz Steps 30 - 87.97500 MHz, 25.0 kHz Steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the digital signal processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitted RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attenuation described below: Pass Band Minimum of 40 dB Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band 30.000 - 42.899 MHz < 1.2 dB 60.0 - 128.7 MHz 42.900 - 61.499 MHz < 1.5 dB 85.8 - 184.5 MHz 61.500 - 87.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 123.0 - 264.0 MHz
Spread Spectrum: This is a SINCGARS ECCM spread spectrum waveform hopping over 2320 channels, with hop rate 111.11 hops/second, dwell time 7.2 milliseconds per hop. System can lock out any/all hop channels.
Max bit rate for 22K0F1D is 16 kbps. Max bit rate for 26K0F1D is 20 kbps.
Modulation Techniques and Coding: FM Voice, FSK Data
Maximum modulation frequency is 3 kHz for FM Voice modes and 8 kHz for FSK Data modes.
Deviation Ratio 1.667 for FM voice, 0.7 for FSK data.

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 FM Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF tuning capabilities are as follows: 30 - 87.995 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 30 - 87.99375 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 30 - 87.99346 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 30 - 87.9875 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 30 - 87.975 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 138 - 150.79488 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 162 - 173.9952 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 225 - 399.93 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 406.1 - 419.99444 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services. In particular, system will inhibit transmissions in the ILS glide slope band 328.6-335.4 MHz.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to inhibit transmissions of emissions 16K0F3E, 18K5F3E, 22K0F1D, and 22K0F3E within the bands 162.0125-174 MHz and 406.1-420 MHz.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the digital signal processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitted RF output is then filtered by a eight band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attenuation described below: Pass Band Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band (dB min) 30.000 - 42.899 MHz < 1.2 dB 60.0 - 128.7 MHz (40dB) 42.900 - 61.499 MHz < 1.5 dB 85.8 - 184.5 MHz (40dB) 61.500 - 87.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 123.0 - 264.0 MHz (40dB) 88.000 - 141.999 MHz < 1.6 dB 176.0 - 426.0 MHz (40dB) 142.000 - 175.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 284.0 - 528.0 MHz (40dB) 176.000 - 244.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 352.4 - 735.0 MHz (40dB) 245.000 - 330.000 MHz < 1.4 dB 450.0 - 990.0 MHz (30dB) 330.001 - 511.999 MHz < 1.5 dB 660.0 - 1536.0 MHz (30dB)
Max bit rate for FSK Data is 16 kbps. Max bit rate for C4FM Digital Voice is 9.6 kbps.
Modulation Techniques and Coding: FM Voice, FSK Data, C4FM Digital Voice
Maximum modulation frequency is 3 kHz for FM Voice modes and 8 kHz for FSK Data mode.
Deviation Ratio: Deviation Ratio for 16K0F3E FM Voice (Max Freq Deviation 5 kHz) is 1.667. Deviation Ratio for 18K5F3E FM Voice (Max Freq Deviation 6.5 kHz) is 2.166. Deviation Ratio for 22K0F3E FM Voice (Max Freq Deviation 8 kHz) is 2.667. Deviation Ratio for 11K0F3E FM Voice (Max Freq Deviation 2.5 kHz) is .833. Deviation Ratio for 22K0F1D FSK Data (Max Freq Deviation 5.6 kHz) is 0.7. Deviation ration for 8K00F1D C4FM Digital Voice (Max Freq Deviation 1.8 kHz) is 0.6.
Harmonic levels -53 dBc in bands 30-88 MHz, 138-150.8 MHz, and 225-400 MHz. Harmonic levels -60 dBc in bands 162.0125-174 MHz and 406.1-420 MHz.

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 CPM Data Mode Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 30 - 87.99500 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 30 - 87.99375 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 30 - 87.99346 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 30 - 87.98750 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 30 - 87.97500 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the digital signal processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitted RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attenuation described below: Pass Band Minimum of 40 dB Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band 30.000 - 42.899 MHz < 1.2 dB 60.0 - 128.7 MHz 42.900 - 61.499 MHz < 1.5 dB 85.8 - 184.5 MHz 61.500 - 87.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 123.0 - 264.0 MHz
Modulation Techniques and Coding: CPM (Continuous Phase Modulation)

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 AM Communications Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF tuning capabilities are as follows: 138 - 150.8 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 138 - 150.79488 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 162 - 173.9952 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 225 - 399.93 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 406.1 - 419.99444 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services. In particular, system will inhibit transmissions in the ILS glide slope band 328.6-335.4 MHz.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to inhibit transmissions of emission 16K0A1D within the bands 162.0125-174 MHz and 406.1-420 MHz.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the digital signal processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitted RF output is then filtered by a five band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attenuation described below: Pass Band Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band(dB min) 88.000 - 141.999 MHz < 1.6 dB 176.0 - 426.0 MHz (40 dB) 142.000 - 175.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 284.0 - 528.0 MHz (40 dB) 176.000 - 244.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 352.4 - 735.0 MHz (40 dB) 245.000 - 330.000 MHz < 1.4 dB 450.0 - 990.0 MHz (30 dB) 330.001 - 511.999 MHz < 1.5 dB 660.0 - 1536.0 MHz(30 dB)
Modulation Techniques and Coding: AM (double sideband) Voice, ASK (Amplitude Shift Keying) Data

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 HAVE QUICK Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: Applies when system is in single channel, unhopping mode. Hopping mode frequencies are determined by algorithm in accordance with HAVE QUICK specs and standards.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services. In particular, system will inhibit transmissions in the ILS glide slope band 328.6 - 335.4 MHz.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the digital signal processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitted RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attention described below:
Pass Band Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band (dB Min) 176.000 - 244.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 352.4 - 735.0 MHz (40 dB) 245.000 - 330.000 MHz < 1.4 dB 450.0 - 990.0 MHz (30 dB) 330.001 - 511.999 MHz < 1.5 dB 660.0 - 1536.0 MHz(30 dB)
Spread Spectrum: This mode uses HAVE QUICK ECCM waveform spread spectrum (frequency hopping).
Modulation Techniques and Coding: AM (double sideband) Voice, ASK (Amplitude Shift Keying) Data

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 MANET Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 225 - 400 MHz, 500 kHz steps 225 - 399 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1370 - 1389.6 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1755 - 1849.8 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 5 kHz steps via the remote control and the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services. In particular, system will inhibit transmissions in the ILS glide slope band 328.6 - 335.4 MHz.
Also, 5M00G1D emission will be inhibited from use in the 225 - 400 MHz band to comply with DOD Channeling Plan.
Emission Designators: The first emission designator 1M20G1D (characteristics listed in left column) refers to mode that uses GMSK modulation and spread spectrum. The second designator 1M20G1D (characteristics listed in center column) refers to mode that uses QPSK modulation.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the Digital Signal Processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitter RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attenuation as shown below: Pass Band Minimum 30 dB Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band 225.00 - 318.00 MHz < 2.5 dB 450.0 - 954.0 MHz 318.00 - 512.00 MHz < 2.5 dB 636.0 - 1536.0 MHz 1350.00 - 1999.99 MHz < 2.5 dB 2700.0 - 6000.0 MHz
Spread Spectrum: For GMSK (emission characterized in left columns) only, a direct sequence spread spectrum is used. Following encoding each group of 3 bits is then used to selected one of 8 possible 16-chip sequences to be sent over air. There is a spreading of 3 encoded bits to 16 on-air GMSK chips.
Max Bit Rate for 1M20G1D (GMSK, left column) is 168.750 kbps; 1M20G1D (QPSK, center column) is 1.18125 Mbps; and for 5M00G1D it is 4.725 Mbps.
Modulation Techniques and Coding: GMSK w/ DSSS (left col 1M20G1D) QPSK (center col 1M20G1D, 5M00G1D) Encoding for 1M20G1D (GMSK, left column): Rate 3/4 forward error correcting (FEC) convolutional encoded, 3 encoded bit then spread to 16 "on-air" chips, 1200000 chips per sec. GMSK modulation parameters: BT=0.3 (L=3). Encoding for 1M20G1D (QPSK, center column): Frame Format of 224 Data Symbols with 32 Known Symbols (256 Symbols) & rate 3/4 FEC convolutional coding. Encoding for 5M00G1D (QPSK): Frame Format of 224 Data Symbols with 32 Known Symbols (256 Symbols) & rate 3/4 FEC convolutional coding.
Emission Chips Spreading Coding Data Designator Per Method Rate Second 1M20G1D 1200000 * 3 bit / 16 chips * 3/4 = 168750 bps
Emission Symbols Frame Coding Modulation Data Designator Per Format (Bits Per Rate Second Symbol) 1M20G1D 900000 * (224/256) * 3/4 * 2 = 1181250 bps 5M00G1D 3600000 * (224/256) * 3/4 * 2 = 4725000 bps
Maximum modulation frequency 3.6 MHz for 5M00G1D, 1.2 MHz for 1M20G1D (GMSK), and 900 kHz for 1M20G1D (QPSK).
Deviation Ratio is .5 for 1M20G1D (GMSK), not defined for 1M20G1D (QPSK) and 5M00G1D emissions.

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 MANET Transmitter #2 Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 225 - 400 MHz, 500 kHz steps 225 - 399 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1370 - 1389.6 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1755 - 1849.8 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 5 kHz steps, via the remote control and the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Within the US&P, system will be configured to transmit only in bands allocated to federal mobile services. In particular, system will inhibit transmissions in the ILS glide slope band 328.6 - 335.4 MHz.
Also, 5M00D1D emission will be inhibited from transmission in the 225 - 400 MHz band to comply with DOD Channeling Plan.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the Digital Signal Processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitter RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attenuation as shown below: Pass Band Minimum 30 dB Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band 225.00 - 318.00 MHz < 2.5 dB 450.0 - 954.0 MHz 318.00 - 512.00 MHz < 2.5 dB 636.0 - 1536.0 MHz 1350.00 - 1999.99 MHz < 2.5 dB 2700.0 - 6000.0 MHz
Max Bit Rate for 1M20D1D is 2.3625 Mbps and for 5M00D1D it is 9.45 Mbps.
Modulation Techniques and Coding: 16-QAM (16-ary Quadrature Amplitude Modulation Encoding for 1M20D1D: Frame Format of 224 Data Symbols with 32 Known Symbols (256 Symbols) & rate 3/4 FEC convolutional coding. Encoding for 5M00D1D: Frame Format of 224 Data Symbols with 32 Known symbols (256 Symbols) & rate 3/4 FEC convolutional coding.
Emission Symbols Frame Coding Modulation Data Designator Per Format (Bits Per Rate Second Symbol) 1M20D1D 900000 * (224/256) * 3/4 * 4 = 2362500 bps 5M00D1D 3600000 * (224/256) * 3/4 * 4 = 9450000 bps
Max Modulation Frequency is 3.6 MHz for 5M00D1D Max Modulation Frequency is 900 kHz for 1M20D1D

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 SATCOM Transmitter Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: RF channeling capabilities are as follows: For 2K40G1D & 4K80G1D: 291.0 MHz, 5 kHz steps For 22K0F1D & 25K0F1D: 291.0 MHz, 25 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps, via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel (10 Hz steps via the front panel for 25K0F1D). This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
SATCOM functions conform to US MIL-STD-188-181B standards for 5 kHz channels (emissions 2K40G1D and 4K80G1D) and for 25 kHz channels (emissions 22K0F1D and 25K0F1D).
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the Digital Signal Processing (DSP) portion of the radio. The transmitter RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with in band insertion loss and attention as shown below: Pass Band Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band (dB min) 176.000 - 244.999 MHz < 1.2 dB 352.4 - 735.0 MHz (40 dB) 245.000 - 330.000 MHz < 1.4 dB 450.0 - 990.0 MHz (30 dB) 330.001 - 511.999 MHz < 1.5 dB 660.0 - 1536.0 MHz(30 dB)
Max Bit Rate for 2K40G1D is 1.2 kbps, Max Bit Rate for 4K80G1D is 2.4 kbps, Max Bit Rate for 22K0F1D is 16 kbps, and Max Bit Rate for 25K0F1D is 56 kbps.
Modulation Techniques and Coding: SBPSK(Shaped BPSK), SFSK(Shaped Frequency Shift Keying)

Transmitter.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Transmitter.Remarks:
AN/PRC-117(V)(C) MUOS Transmitter Remarks:
Note: the 5M00G7W emission designator is defined by the MUOS system per SPS-18099/2
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: Software controlled step size (channeling plans) are defined at 5.0 MHz for MUOS and should be set to 302.5, 307.5, 312.5, or 317.5 MHz. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Output Filter: The transmitter baseband signal is filtered and conditioned by the Digital Signal Processing (DSP) portion of the radio.
The transmitter RF output is then filtered by a three band, low pass, elliptical filter with insertion loss and attenuation as shown below.
Band Pass Band Pass Band Insertion Loss Attenuation Band(dB min) 6 176-244.999 MHz <1.2 dB 352.4-735MHz (40 dB) 7 245-330 MHz <1.4 dB 450-990 MHz (30 dB) 8 330.001-511.999 MHz <1.5 dB 660-1536 MHz (30 dB)
Modulation Techniques and Coding: Digital chip rate: 3.84 Mcps; Data rate: 64kbps; spread code repetition rate: 10 ms; Coding gain 15 dB

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 SINCGARS Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 30 - 87.99500 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 30 - 87.99375 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 30 - 87.99346 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 30 - 87.98750 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 30 - 87.97500 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 10%, -20 dB BW = 36% & -60 dB BW = 150% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 30.000 MHz 87.999 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 3.00 MHz 8.79 MHz -20dB: 10.80 MHz 31.68 MHz -60dB: 45.00 MHz 132.00 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria with input at 8 kHz deviation is 10 dB SINAD for FM Voice and for FSK Data criteria is 90% Packet Success.

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 FM Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF tuning capabilities are as follows: 30 - 87.995 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 30 - 87.99375 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 30 - 87.99346 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 30 - 87.9875 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 30 - 87.975 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 138 - 150.79488 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 162 - 173.9952 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 225 - 399.93 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 406.1 - 419.99444 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 10%, -20 dB BW = 36% & -60 dB BW = 150% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 30.000 MHz 420.000 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 3.00 MHz 42.00 MHz -20dB: 10.80 MHz 151.20 MHz -60dB: 45.00 MHz 630.00 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria is 10 dB SINAD for FM Voice (w/ freq deviation of 8 kHz). For FSK Data & C4FM Data it is 90% packet success.
Note: In the bands 162.0125-174 MHz and 406.1-420 MHz, in accordance with NTIA 5.3.5.2 requirements the following additional receiver data is submitted:
Band (MHz): 162.0125-174 406.1-420 Adjacent channel selectivity (dB): 49 46 Inter-modulation rejection (dB): 70 70 Conducted spurious emissions (dBm): -73 -73

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 CPM Data Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 30 - 87.99500 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 30 - 87.99375 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 30 - 87.99346 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 30 - 87.98750 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 30 - 87.97500 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 10%, -20 dB BW = 36% & -60 dB BW = 150% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 30.000 MHz 87.999 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 3.00 MHz 8.79 MHz -20dB: 10.80 MHz 31.68 MHz -60dB: 45.00 MHz 132.00 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria: 90% Packet Success

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 AM Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF tuning capabilities are as follows: 138 - 150.8 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 138 - 150.79488 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 138 - 150.8 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 162 - 173.9952 MHz , 8.33 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 162 - 174 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 225 - 399.93 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 5.0 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 6.25 kHz steps 406.1 - 419.99444 MHz, 8.33 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 12.5 kHz steps 406.1 - 420 MHz, 25.0 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 10%, -20 dB BW = 36% & -60 dB BW = 150% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 138.000 MHz 420.000 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 13.80 MHz 42.00 MHz -20dB: 49.70 MHz 151.20 MHz -60dB: 207.00 MHz 630.00 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria for AM Voice (6K00A3E) is 10 dB SINAD and for ASK Data (16K0A1D) criteria is 90% packet success.

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 HAVE QUICK Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: Applies when system is in single channel, unhopping mode. Hopping mode frequencies are determined by algorithm in accordance with HAVE QUICK specs and standards.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 10%, -20 dB BW = 36% & -60 dB BW = 150% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 225.000 MHz 400.000 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 22.50 MHz 40.00 MHz -20dB: 81.00 MHz 144.00 MHz -60dB: 337.50 MHz 600.00 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria for AM Voice (6K00A3E) is 10 dB SINAD and for ASK Data (16K0A1D) criteria is 90% packet success.

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 MANET Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 225 - 400 MHz, 500 kHz steps 225 - 399 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1370 - 1389.6 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1755 - 1849.8 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 5 kHz steps via the remote control and the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Emission designator 5M00G1D not utilized in 225 - 400 MHz band.
Emission Designators: 1M20G1D is emission designator for both GMSK modulated spread spectrum mode and QPSK modulated mode.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 12.9%, -20 dB BW = 42% & -60 dB BW = 115% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 225.000 MHz 1850.000 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 29.025 MHz 238.650 MHz -20dB: 94.500 MHz 777.000 MHz -60dB: 258.750 MHz 2127.500 MHz
Sensitivity is -105 dBm for emission designator 1M20G1D (GMSK). Sensitivity is -91 dBm for emission designator 1M20G1D (QPSK). Sensitivity is -85 dBm for emission designator 5M00G1D (QPSK).
Sensitivity Criteria: 90% Packet Success

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 MANET Receiver #2 Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
Tuning range/RF channeling capabilities are as follows: 225 - 400 MHz, 500 kHz steps 225 - 399 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 225 - 400 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1370 - 1389.6 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1370 - 1390 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 500 kHz steps 1755 - 1849.8 MHz, 1.2 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 2.5 MHz steps 1755 - 1850 MHz, 5.0 MHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 5 kHz steps via the remote control and the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
Emission Designator 5M00D1D not operated in band 225 - 400 MHz.
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 12.9%, -20 dB BW = 42% & -60 dB BW = 115% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 225.000 MHz 1850.000 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 29.025 MHz 238.650 MHz -20dB: 94.500 MHz 777.000 MHz -60dB: 258.750 MHz 2127.500 MHz
Sensitivity is -85 dBm for emission designator 1M20D1D (16-QAM). Sensitivity is -79 dBm for emission designator 5M00D1D (16-QAM).
Sensitivity Criteria: 90% Packet Success

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209 SATCOM Receiver Remarks:
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: RF channeling capabilities are as follows: For 2K40G1D & 4K80G1D: 243.0 MHz, 5 kHz steps For 22K0F1D & 25K0F1D: 243.0 MHz, 25 kHz steps
Software controlled step sizes (channeling plans) are defined for each emission designator (mode) in order to comply with the established operating standards of each mode. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel (10 Hz steps via the front panel for 25K0F1D). This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
SATCOM functions conform to US MIL-STD-188-181B standards for 5 kHz channels (emissions 2K40G1D and 4K80G1D) and for 25 kHz channels (emissions 22K0F1D and 25K0F1D).
RF Selectivity: The bandwidth of the varactor tuning preselector is determined by the receive frequency. The filter bandwidth is a percentage of the receive frequency. The -3 dB BW = 10%, -20 dB BW = 36%, & -60 dB BW = 150% of the receive frequency. Below is the RF selectivity over this mode's tuning range:
Received RF Frequency: 243.000 MHz 270.000 MHz RF Selectivity BW @ -3dB: 24.30 MHz 27.00 MHz -20dB: 87.48 MHz 97.20 MHz -60dB: 364.50 MHz 405.00 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria: 90% Packet Success

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
MPE-S GB-GRAM GPS Receiver Remarks:
Nomenclature, Manufacturer's Name and Model No.: The AN/PRC-209 uses an OEM equipment Rockwell International MPE-S GPS module, designed to the specifications in MIL-PRF-GB-GRAM-300.
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship, or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: Fixed, 2 Channels; receiver tunes in both L1 and L2 simultaneously.
Oscillator Tuned: Oscillator is tuned above tuned frequency for L2 and below tuned frequency for L1.

Receiver.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Receiver.Remarks:
AN/PRC-209(V) (C) MUOS Receiver Remarks:
Note: the 5M00G7W emission designator is defined by the MUOS system per SPS-18099/2
Installation: Operator may wear manpack radio, or carry it onto any of the following platforms: aircraft, ship or land vehicle.
RF Channeling Capability: Software controlled step size (channeling plans) are defined at 5.0 MHz for MUOS and should be set to 362.5, 367.5, 312.5, or 372.5 or 377.5 MHz. Radio hardware is capable of tuning in 1 Hz steps via the remote control, and 100 Hz steps via the front panel. This capability exists to accommodate potential future waveforms.
RF Selectivity: RF selectivity values are actually percentages of receive frequency in MHz, as follows: -3 dB: 12.9% of receive frequency -20 dB: 42 % of receive frequency -60 dB: 115% of receive frequency
Values shown in data fields above correspond to the maximum receive frequency of 377.5 MHz
Sensitivity Criteria: 90% packet success

Note: magnetic mount, vehicular GPS antenna

Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Main Beam Gain: Minimum: 14 dBi Mid-Band: 16 dBi Maximum: 17 dBi

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Main Beam Gain: -6.5 dBi @ 30 MHz 1.5 dBi @ 88 MHz 5.0 dBi @ 108 MHz 3.0 dBi @ 174 MHz 2.0 dBi @ 225 MHz 0.0 dBi @ 450-512 MHz

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Main Beam Gain -5 dBi @225-450 MHz, -5 dBi @ 1350 - 1550 MHz, -15 dBi at all other frequencies

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Main Beam Gain Frequency Gain 500 - 800 -3 dBi 800 - 1000 MHz 0 dBi 1 - 1.8 MHz 1 dBi 1.8 - 2.5 MHz 0 dBi

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
Frequency(s): 30 - 88 MHz (VHF) 108 - 512 MHz (UHF) 550 - 2000 MHz (High band)
Antenna Type: The RF-3187-AT320/4 is a vehicular multiband whip antenna. It operates over three bands: VHF, UHF, and High Band. The High Band dipole is located at the whip tip. the lower dipole covers 30 - 512 MHz and is diplexed allowing for two feeds (VHF and UHF)
Main Beam Gain: Frequency Band Gain 30 88 MHz -8 dBi 108 - 512 MHz -1 dBi 550 - 2000 MHz -0 dBi (All gains shown are minimum main beam gain over the respective frequency ranges)

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
Note: Installed on teh-E-*C (Joint Stars)
Nomenclature, Manufacturer's Model No.: VHF/UHF/L&S-Band Antenna
Frequency(s): Antenna is built for 30 - 88 MHz, 108 - 174 MHz, and 800 -2650 MHz bands
Antenna Type: Omni-directional antenna with null at 90 degrees in elevation
Main Beam Gain: -22 dBi max @ 30 MHz, -19 dBi max @ 88 MHz, -7 dBi max @ 225 MHz, -4 dBi max @ 300 MHz, -5 dBi max @ 400 MHz.

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
Nomenclature, Manufacturer's Model No., Antenna Type: U B Corp P/N: A01415-1 (aluminum model) or A01415S-1 (stainless steel model) telemetry blade antennas. NSN 5985-01-010-7682.
Main Beam Gain, Beamwidth: gain and beamwidth details for both models are as follows:
A01415-1 (aluminum model) Axis Frequency Maximum Gain (dBi) Beam Plane Beamwidth (deg) Pitch Low (1350 MHz) 57.3 Elevation 32.32 Pitch High (1900 MHz) 4.76 Elevation 70.06 Roll Low (1350 MHz) 5.43 Elevation 30.65 Roll High (1900 MHz) 5.60 Elevation 26.47 Yaw Low (1350 MHz) -0.43 Azimuth 360(approx) Yaw High (1900 MHz) 0 (approx) Azimuth 360 (approx)
A01415S-1 (stainless steel model) Axis Frequency Maximum Gain (dBi) Beam Plane Beamwidth (deg) Pitch Low (1350 MHz) 5.74 Elevation 32.62 Pitch High (1900 MHz) 4.78 Elevation 64 (approx) Roll Low (1350 MHz) 5.29 Elevation 31.00 Roll High (1900 MHz) 5.5 (approx) Elevation 30 (approx) Yaw Low (1350 MHz) -0.52 Azimuth 360(approx) Yaw High (1900 MHz) 0 (approx) Azimuth 360 (approx)
**Values labled as approximate were obtained via inspection of antenna plots provided by the vendor; all other values are numerical figures provided directly from the vendor

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
Frequency(s): Band 1: 225 - 450 MHz (UHF) (port 1) Band 2: 2200 - 2500 MHz (S-Band) (port 2)
Main Beam Gain: Band 1: 1 dBi Band 2: 4 dBi
1st Major Side Lobe: Band 1: NA, Band 2: -1 dBi at 30Â° elevation
Horizontal Beamwidth: Band 1: 360Â° Azimuth Band 2: 360Â° Azimuth
Vertical Beamwidth: Band 1: 60Â° Elevation Band 2: 30Â° Elevation

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Main Beam Gain: Frequency Gain 30 MHz -26.0 dBi (lowest gain) 68 MHz -15.0 dBi 108-118 MHz -10.0 dBi 118-152 MHz -6.5 dBi 400 MHz 0.0 dBi (highest gain)

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Note: Model 19-440-10 VHF/UHF SATCOM antenna has two internal antennas. SATCOM is the top circular turnstile 4 horizonal dipole antenna of the 19-440-10 VHF/UHF SATCOM antenna

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
NOTE: This is MUOS Antenna that attaches to the Manpack via diplexer and gooseneck
Antenna Type: Quadfiller Helix with Hybird feed

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
Directional aluminum mast with 6-element helix. This is a MUOS antenna that attaches to the manpack via a diplexer for fixed site installations.
1st Major Side Lobe: -13.8 dB suppression
Typical Main Beam Gain over frequency range: Frequency Gain 225 MHz 8.5 dBic 250 MHz 9.4 dBic 310 MHz 10.7 dBic 400 MHz 11.9 dBic
NUMBER OF ELEMENTS (TURNS) - 6 OVERALL LENGTH â€“ 84â€ HELICAL DIAMETER - 11.85â€ GROUND PLANE DIAMETER â€“ 35â€ MOUNTING STUB LENGTH â€“ 23â€ ANTENNA WEIGHT (lbs.) 34

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks:
This is a MUOS antenna that attaches to the manpack via a diplexer for fixed site installations.
Typical Main Beam Gain over frequency range: Frequency Gain 240 MHz +12 dBiC 270 MHz +10 dBiC 290 MHz +10.6 dBiC 320 MHz +9 dBiC 350 MHz +9 dBiC 375 MHz +10.9 dBiC 400 MHz +12.9 dBiC

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Antenna Horizontal/Vertical Beamwidth: 225 MHz: 62 deg 312 MHz: 44 deg 400 MHz: 33 deg Vertical and horizontal beamwidths are within 1 degree of each other.

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Typical Main Beam Gain over frequency range: Frequency Gain .24GHz -2dBi .29GHz -1dBi .32GHz -.8dBi .36GHz -3dBi .38GHz -2.5dBi

Antenna.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
Antenna.Remarks: Main Beam Gain: -6 dBi @ 30 MHz 2.0 dBi @ 88 MHz 5.0 dBi @ 116 MHz 3.0 dBi @ 512 MHz

SSRequest.legacyReleasability: Not releasable outside the US Government IAW Section 552(b)(1) of Title 5 of the US Code.
SSRequest.Remarks:
*Full name of the system is "AN/PRC-209, Lumen RF-2000 Portable Manpack Radio Set, RF 7800-M-MP Multiband Networking Radio, AN/MRC-145B."
Frequency Requirements, Station Classes: This radio is capable of operations in all listed frequency bands; however, frequency exclusion bands can be implemented via a software programming mode to ensure that the radio is operated in accordance with NTIA regulations. Up to 99 frequency exclusion bands can be programmed. Thus the radio employs preset modulation waveforms according to applicable standards and only in the authorized bands.
Frequencies Station Classes Emission Designators 30 - 30.56 MHz, FL, MO 16K0F3E (FM Voice/SINCGARS FM Voice) 32 - 33 MHz, 18K5F3E (FM Voice) 34 - 35 MHz, 22K0F3E (FM Voice) 36 - 37 MHz, 22K0F1D (FSK Data/SINCGARS FSK Data) 38.25 - 39 MHz, 26K0F1D (SINCGARS FSK Data) 40 - 42 MHz, 11K0F3E (FM Voice) 46.6 - 47 MHz, 8K00F1D (C4FM Digital Voice) 49.6 - 50 MHz 25K0G1D (CPM Data)
138 - 144 MHz, FL, MO 16K0F3E (FM Voice) 148 - 149.9 MHz, 18K5F3E (FM Voice) 150.05 - 150.8 MHz 22K0F3E (FM Voice) 22K0F1D (FSK Data) 11K0F3E (FM Voice) 8K00F1D (C4FM Digital Voice) 6K00A3E (AM Voice) 16K0A1D (ASK Data) **Transmissions in 138-144 MHz, 148-149.9 MHz, and 150.05-150.8 MHz for tactical and training operations only.
162.0125 - 173.2 MHz, FL, MO 11K0F3E (FM Voice) 173.4 - 174 MHz 8K00F1D (C4FM Digital Voice) 6K00A3E (AM Voice) **Transmissions in 162.0125-173.2 MHz and 173.4-174 MHz for non-tactical, intra-base communications only.
225 - 328.6 MHz, FL, MO 16K0F3E (FM Voice) 335.4 - 399.9 MHz 18K5F3E (FM Voice) 22K0F3E (FM Voice) 22K0F1D (FSK Data) 11K0F3E (FM Voice) 8K00F1D (C4FM Digital Voice) 6K00A3E (AM Voice/HAVE QUICK AM Voice) 7K00A3E (AM Voice/HAVE QUICK AM Voice) 16K0A1D (ASK Data/HAVE QUICK ASK Data) 1M20G1D (MANET GMSK/QPSK Data) 1M20D1D (MANET 16-QAM Data) **Radio will be configured to inhibit transmission in the 328.6-335.4 MHz ILS Glide Slope band.
406.1 - 420 MHz FL, MO 11K0F3E (FM Voice) 8K00F1D (C4FM Digital Voice) 6K00A3E (AM Voice) **Transmissions in 406.1-420 MHz for non-tactical, intra-base communications only.
1370 - 1390 MHz FL, MO 1M20G1D (MANET GMSK/QPSK Data) 5M00G1D (MANET QPSK Data) 1M20D1D (MANET 16-QAM Data) 5M00D1D (MANET 16-QAM Data)
1755 - 1780 MHz ML, MS 1M20G1D (MANET GMSK/QPSK Data) 1M20D1D (MANET 16-QAM Data)
1780 - 1850 MHz FL, MO 1M20G1D (MANET GMSK/QPSK Data) 5M00G1D (MANET QPSK Data) 1M20D1D (MANET 16-QAM Data) 5M00D1D (MANET 16-QAM Data)
243 - 270 MHz UA, VA, 2K40G1D (SATCOM SBPSK Data) (Receive Only) TU, TY, TB, 4K80G1D (SATCOM SBPSK Data) TI, TG, TJ 22K0F1D (SATCOM FSK Data) 25K0F1D (SATCOM FSK Data)
291 - 318 MHz UA, VA, 2K40G1D (SATCOM SBPSK Data) (Transmit Only) TU, TY, TB, 4K80G1D (SATCOM SBPSK Data) TI, TG, TJ 22K0F1D (SATCOM FSK Data) 25K0F1D (SATCOM FSK Data)
300 - 320 MHz UA, VA, 5M00G7W (MUOS) (Transmit Only) TU, TY, TB, TI, TG, TJ
360 - 380 MHz UA, VA, 5M00G7W (MUOS) (Receive Only) TU, TY, TB, TI, TG, TJ
Type of Service(s) for Stage 4:
MOBILE MOBILE-SATELLITE
Station Class(es) for Stage 4:
FL MO ML MS UA VA TG TJ TB TU TY TI
Target Date - Application Approval: ASAP
Target Date - System Activation: ASAP
AN/PRC-209 Radio used on land (manpack, can be carried onto land vehicle)
AN/PRC-209 Radio used on water (carried onto some kind of maritime vessel)
AN/PRC-209 Radio used in the air (carried onto aircraft)
AN/PRC-209 Radio used to communicate with SATCOM terminal onboard satellite
AN/PRC-209 Radio used to communicate with SATCOM terminal onboard satellite
AN/PRC-209 Radio used to communicate with SATCOM terminal onboard satellite
SSRequest.Title: AN/PRC-209, Lumen RF-2000 Portable Manpack Radio Set, (See Remarks)
SSRequest.Requirement: For the transmission and reception of clear and encrypted voice and data communications by single channel and frequency hopping line of sight (LOS) links, SATCOM links, and mobile ad-hoc networking (MANET) links.
SSRequest.ReplacementInfo: Replacing AN/PRC-117F over timeframe of at least three years from system activation date.
SSRequest.InfoTransferRequirement: AM & FM Voice, ASK Data (16 kbps), FSK Data (56 kbps), CPM Data (64 kbps), GMSK Data (168.75 kbps), QPSK Data (4.725 Mbps), 16-QAM Data (9.45 Mbps), & SBPSK Data (2.4 kbps).
SSRequest.SysRelationEssential: Required for tactical communication and coordination between USAF STS (Special Tactics Squadrons) or TACP (Tactical Air Control Party) units and SOF or Army ground elements being supported. Coordination and direction of USAF Close Air Support aircraft and SOF or Army helicopters. Facilitates SATCOM beyond LOS communication with higher level command units. For dissemination of target, reconnaissance, and situational awareness (SA) data and images (stills and video). Also required for non-tactical, intra-base mobile communications.

POCInformation.Description: AF Frequency Management Agency AFFMA/DON 2461 Eisenhower Ave., Suite 1203 Alexandria, VA 22331-1500
Aeronautical Systems Center (AFMC) 88 CG/SCXI (ASC 08024) Building 47, Area B, 2690 K Street Wright-Patterson AFB, OH 45433-7661
